title: Blind SQL Injection
description: A search feature that doesn't return results directly but behaves differently if a record exists.

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Identify the vulnerability and explain why it is 'blind'.
stages.target: The query is vulnerable to SQL injection, but the application only returns a boolean-like response (found/not found), not the data itself.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: Provide a payload to check if the first letter of the admin's password is 'A'.
stages.target: admin' AND (SELECT SUBSTR(password,1,1) FROM users WHERE username='admin') = 'A

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: How would you fix this using parameterized queries?

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: Explain how an attacker can still extract data even if it's not displayed on the page.
stages.target: Attacker uses Boolean-based or Time-based techniques to ask the database true/false questions and infer data bit by bit.

hints: Notice that the application only tells you if a product exists or not.
Think about using subqueries and conditional logic in SQL.
